title: The Mini
blurb: A 5×5 with no black cells — every letter checked twice. Five across words and five down words, fully interlocked: a double word square.
note: Generated by the complete backtracking search over a quality-filtered lexicon (mini 5 90). Every one of the ten words scores ≥ 90 on the curated crossword list, and all ten are distinct — no symmetric-square cheat.

title: The Blocked Mini
blurb: A 5×5 with four black cells. The layout was not drawn by hand — you give the generator a black-cell count and it searches the legal grids.
note: The four blacks are a rotation-symmetric, fully-checked, connected layout found by complete search from the number 4 alone (generate 5 5 4). The fill then solves that layout above the bar — both stages complete, so “no grid” would be a theorem, not a timeout.

title: The Freeform Mini
blurb: The same 5×5, four-black search as *The Blocked Mini* — but with the 180° symmetry requirement switched off. The blacks fall where the grid wants them, not in mirrored pairs, and the whole layout tilts.
note: The same count-driven complete search as its neighbour, with one constraint lifted: pass *--nonsymmetric* and the layout is no longer restricted to 180°-rotation orbits, so the four blacks land wherever a legal, connected, fully-checked grid allows. Symmetry was always a default-on toggle here, not a hardwired rule (D13). The search stays complete and the fill still solves it above the bar, every entry distinct — only the mirror is gone.

title: The Midi
blurb: A 10×10 built entirely from words of length 3–5. Black cells are placed to hold every entry short, so a big grid fills from a small word list.
note: The layout comes from the cap-driven search: fix the *maximum* entry length (here 5) and let tactically placed blacks keep every run in range. That is what lets a 10×10 fill from the same 2–5-letter lists a mini uses — no ten-letter word lists needed (generate 10 10 0 66 1 --max-len 5).

across.clue: 'I didn't need to know that,' in a text
Swiss mathematician with a number named for him
Remark not meant for all ears

title: The Freeform Midi
blurb: The length-capped 10×10 again, symmetry off. Without the mirror constraint the black cells drift into a lopsided, freeform arrangement — the older, less formal crossword look, and a noticeably different feel to solve.
note: Identical to *The Midi* — cap-driven search, every entry 3–5 letters, layout from the number alone — except the 180° constraint is dropped (*--nonsymmetric*). Turning symmetry off widens the layout search from rotation orbits to every cell independently; the same completeness and distinctness guarantees hold, so this is the identical machine with one aesthetic rule removed, not a weaker one.

across.clue: They may be sealed with a kiss
German industrial city in the Ruhr

down.clue: "I can't believe it!" in a text
Musical with "Don't Cry for Me Argentina"

title: The Field
blurb: Another 10×10 — but this one’s black-cell pattern was *sampled* from an energy field, not searched. Spread-out blacks, and never a 2×2 block.
note: The black-cell layout is drawn by an annealed-Gibbs sampler over the grid field, with the crossword aesthetics written as energy terms: a density spring, an anti-cluster penalty, and an explicit no-2×2-black-block rule it is guaranteed to satisfy. The complete search wins on speed; the field wins on texture (generate 10 10 0 63 1 --max-len 5 --gibbs).

title: The Long Midi
blurb: A 9×9 whose length cap is lifted from the mini’s five to *six*, so several entries — RENDER, APIARY, VENEER, GRATES — run longer than any word a Mini can hold. The layout is a freeform, asymmetric one, sampled from the field.
note: Two independent levers. The *cap* is raised to 6, which lets longer entries in — and that costs nothing but vocabulary: the word lists run to length 15 (D36), so “how long a word can we hold” is a data axis, wholly separate from the double-square *order* that governs a dense mini. The *layout* is drawn by the same annealed-Gibbs field as *The Field*, but with 180° symmetry switched off, so the black cells fall freeform while still guaranteeing no 2×2 block. A bigger, more open grid built from slightly longer words, laid out asymmetrically.

down.clue: Golf tournament, informally, or a skirt
